description: Platform-layer Analytics Service: SQS event ingestion, 13 typed handlers, 8 rollup tables, 9 named metric REST endpoints, generic query DSL, BullMQ partition maintenance, admin recompute. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-02-analytics-service-updated-design.md

userStories.title: Add event_id field to OrthoEvent type in @ortho/event-bus
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a stable per-event UUID on OrthoEvent so that Analytics Service handlers can use it as a deduplication key for analytics_events.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/@ortho/event-bus/src/types.ts: add optional field event_id?: string to the OrthoEvent interface
packages/@ortho/event-bus/src/index.ts still exports OrthoEvent cleanly
Existing event-bus tests still pass (event_id is optional — no breaking change)
Typecheck passes in packages/@ortho/event-bus
userStories.notes: Make the field optional (event_id?: string) to keep backward compat with publishers that don't yet set it. Analytics handlers will fall back to randomUUID() if event_id is absent.

userStories.title: Project scaffolding — package.json, tsconfig.json, Dockerfile
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the apps/platform/analytics/ service directory wired up with correct tooling so that subsequent stories can add code that builds, typechecks, and runs tests.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/package.json exists with name @platform/analytics, type module, Node >=24
scripts: build (tsc), dev (tsx watch src/index.ts), start (node dist/index.js), test (vitest run), typecheck (tsc --noEmit)

Dependencies include: fastify ^5, @fastify/sensible ^6, @fastify/rate-limit, @sinclair/typebox ^0.34, pg ^8, knex ^3, bullmq ^5, ioredis ^5, p-limit ^6, @ortho/event-bus (file:../../../packages/@ortho/event-bus), @ortho/logger (file:../../../packages/@ortho/logger)
devDependencies include: typescript ^5, tsx ^4, vitest ^2, @types/node ^22, @types/pg ^8
apps/platform/analytics/tsconfig.json matches AI service: target ES2022, module NodeNext, moduleResolution NodeNext, strict true, outDir dist, rootDir src
apps/platform/analytics/Dockerfile matches AI service pattern (node:24-alpine, npm ci --omit=dev, CMD node dist/index.js)
npm install completes without errors
Typecheck passes (no src files yet, just empty src/index.ts placeholder)
userStories.notes: Mirror the AI service (apps/platform/ai) package.json and tsconfig.json structure. Add @aws-sdk/client-sqs as a dependency (used by EventBridgeDriver transitively) plus ioredis for BullMQ. The @fastify/rate-limit package provides per-route rate limiting.

userStories.title: Database migration — analytics_events table with monthly partitioning
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the analytics_events immutable raw event log table created with partitioning and indexes so that handlers can write and deduplicate events.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/migrations/001_create_analytics_events.ts exists
up() creates schema platform_analytics if not exists

up() creates analytics_events partitioned by RANGE on occurred_at with columns: id uuid PK gen_random_uuid(), event_id text UNIQUE NOT NULL, event_type text NOT NULL, source text NOT NULL, entity_type text, entity_id text, dimensions jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}', properties jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}', occurred_at timestamptz NOT NULL, ingested_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
up() creates analytics_events_default default partition to catch rows before named partitions exist
up() creates index on (event_type, occurred_at)
up() creates GIN index on dimensions
down() drops the table and default partition
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use knex.raw() for the PARTITION BY RANGE DDL and for creating the default partition — Knex schema builder doesn't support native partitioning. Table goes in platform_analytics schema. The UNIQUE constraint on event_id is the deduplication anchor.

userStories.title: Database migrations — 8 rollup tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all eight daily rollup tables created so that event handlers can atomically increment metrics alongside the raw event insert.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/migrations/002_create_rollup_tables.ts exists
Creates metrics_leads_daily: date, location_id, channel, count int DEFAULT 0, archived int DEFAULT 0, UNIQUE(date, location_id, channel)
Creates metrics_pipeline_daily: date, location_id, pipeline, stage, entries int DEFAULT 0, UNIQUE(date, location_id, pipeline, stage)
Creates metrics_conversions_daily: date, location_id, channel, count int DEFAULT 0, UNIQUE(date, location_id, channel)
Creates metrics_messages_daily: date, location_id, delivered int DEFAULT 0, failed int DEFAULT 0, opt_outs int DEFAULT 0, UNIQUE(date, location_id)

Creates metrics_ad_spend_daily: date, platform, location_id, campaign_id, campaign_name text, impressions int DEFAULT 0, clicks int DEFAULT 0, spend numeric(12,2) DEFAULT 0, UNIQUE(date, platform, location_id, campaign_id)
Creates metrics_campaigns_daily: date, campaign_id, location_id, sent int DEFAULT 0, delivered int DEFAULT 0, opened int DEFAULT 0, clicked int DEFAULT 0, UNIQUE(date, campaign_id, location_id)
Creates metrics_referrals_daily: date, location_id, count int DEFAULT 0, UNIQUE(date, location_id)

Creates metrics_coordinators_daily: date, location_id, coordinator_id, response_time_sum int DEFAULT 0, response_time_count int DEFAULT 0, time_in_stage_sum int DEFAULT 0, time_in_stage_count int DEFAULT 0, UNIQUE(date, location_id, coordinator_id)
All tables created in platform_analytics schema
down() drops all 8 tables
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add a comment in the migration for metrics_ad_spend_daily explaining that campaign_name is a display hint only — queries must always group by campaign_id, never campaign_name. All tables are in platform_analytics schema.

userStories.title: Repositories — events.ts (raw insert + dedup) and rollups.ts (upsert helpers)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed repository functions for inserting events and upserting rollup rows so that handlers can share consistent SQL patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/repositories/events.ts exports insertEvent(client: PoolClient, params: InsertEventParams): Promise<{ inserted: boolean }> using INSERT INTO platform_analytics.analytics_events ... ON CONFLICT (event_id) DO NOTHING, returning true if a row was inserted

apps/platform/analytics/src/repositories/rollups.ts exports one typed upsert function per rollup table (8 total): upsertLeadDaily, upsertPipelineDaily, upsertConversionDaily, upsertMessageDaily, upsertAdSpendDaily, upsertCampaignDaily, upsertReferralDaily, upsertCoordinatorDaily
Each upsert function accepts a pg PoolClient (for transaction use) and typed params; uses INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET col = col + EXCLUDED.col for increment tables
upsertAdSpendDaily uses ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE to overwrite the full row (spend, clicks, impressions, campaign_name) rather than incrementing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use raw pg PoolClient (not Knex) for the transaction-level operations so handlers can join inserts and upserts in a single BEGIN/COMMIT block. The insertEvent return value (inserted: boolean) is the idempotency gate: handlers skip the rollup write when inserted=false.

userStories.title: Event handlers — lead-created, lead-archived, lead-converted
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the three lead lifecycle handlers that write to analytics_events and the lead/conversion rollup tables so that lead acquisition metrics are captured.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/lead-created.ts: extracts location_id and channel from payload, writes analytics_events row (entity_type='lead') and increments metrics_leads_daily.count in one transaction; skips rollup if event was a duplicate
apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/lead-archived.ts: extracts location_id from payload, uses channel='unknown', increments metrics_leads_daily.archived; skips rollup on duplicate
apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/lead-converted.ts: extracts location_id and channel from payload, increments metrics_conversions_daily.count; skips rollup on duplicate
Each handler signature is (event: OrthoEvent, pool: Pool) => Promise<void>
Each handler opens a pg transaction (pool.connect() + BEGIN/COMMIT), calls insertEvent, checks inserted flag before rollup write
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Event handler — stage-changed (dual rollup: pipeline + coordinators)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the StageChangedHandler that atomically writes to both metrics_pipeline_daily and metrics_coordinators_daily so that funnel and coordinator performance metrics are captured in one transaction.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/stage-changed.ts exists
Extracts location_id, pipeline, stage_to, triggered_by?, response_time_seconds?, time_in_stage_seconds from payload

Opens one DB transaction: insertEvent → if inserted=true, upsertPipelineDaily (entries+1) AND if triggered_by non-null: upsertCoordinatorDaily (always increments time_in_stage_sum/count; increments response_time_sum/count only when response_time_seconds is present)
If insertEvent returns inserted=false (duplicate), both rollup writes are skipped in the same transaction
Events without triggered_by do NOT write to metrics_coordinators_daily
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the most complex handler. The dual-skip idempotency (both pipeline and coordinator rollups skip on duplicate) is spec-confirmed (Q8 Answer C). Keep the transaction single: one BEGIN wrapping insertEvent + both upserts.

userStories.title: Event handlers — message-delivered, message-failed, opt-out-received
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the three messaging handlers that update metrics_messages_daily so that delivery reliability and opt-out metrics are tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/message-delivered.ts: extracts location_id, increments metrics_messages_daily.delivered by 1; skips on duplicate event_id
apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/message-failed.ts: extracts location_id, increments metrics_messages_daily.failed by 1; skips on duplicate event_id
apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/opt-out-received.ts: extracts location_id, increments metrics_messages_daily.opt_outs by 1; skips on duplicate event_id
All three use same transaction pattern: insertEvent → check inserted → upsert
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Event handlers — campaign-sent, campaign-delivered, email-opened, email-clicked
userStories.description: As a developer, I need four handlers that update metrics_campaigns_daily so that email broadcast performance metrics (sent, delivered, opened, clicked) are tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/campaign-sent.ts: extracts campaign_id, location_id, increments metrics_campaigns_daily.sent by 1; skips on duplicate

apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/campaign-delivered.ts: extracts campaign_id, location_id, recipient_count from payload, increments metrics_campaigns_daily.delivered by recipient_count (not 1); skips on duplicate
apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/email-opened.ts: extracts campaign_id, location_id, increments metrics_campaigns_daily.opened by 1; skips on duplicate
apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/email-clicked.ts: extracts campaign_id, location_id, increments metrics_campaigns_daily.clicked by 1; skips on duplicate
All four use the standard insertEvent + inserted-gate pattern
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: campaign-delivered increments by recipient_count (bulk delivery confirmation from SendGrid webhook). Standard idempotency applies — if event_id is a duplicate, the rollup increment is also skipped.

userStories.title: Event handlers — referral-converted (dual rollup) and ad-spend-synced (relaxed idempotency)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the referral and ad-spend handlers, which have non-standard rollup behaviour, so that referral attribution and ad performance data are correctly stored.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/referral-converted.ts: extracts location_id, writes to both metrics_referrals_daily (count+1) and metrics_conversions_daily (channel='referral', count+1) in one transaction; both rollup writes are skipped if event_id is a duplicate

apps/platform/analytics/src/handlers/ad-spend-synced.ts: extracts platform, location_id, synced_date from top-level payload; iterates records[] array; for each record upserts metrics_ad_spend_daily with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE overwriting spend/clicks/impressions/campaign_name; the rollup upsert executes even if the analytics_events insert was skipped (relaxed idempotency — allows re-sync corrections)
ad-spend-synced.ts has a comment explaining the relaxed idempotency rule and why it differs from other handlers
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: ad-spend relaxed idempotency: the upsertAdSpendDaily call always runs regardless of whether insertEvent returned inserted=true or false. This allows Integration Hub to re-publish a corrected spend figure with the same event_id. See spec Section 4 and 6.1.

userStories.title: Event router + SQS consumer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need event-router.ts and sqs-consumer.ts so that incoming SQS messages are routed to the correct typed handler with p-limit concurrency control.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/services/event-router.ts exports routeEvent(event: OrthoEvent, pool: Pool): Promise<void> using a switch on event.event_type that calls the appropriate handler; unknown event types are logged at debug level and resolved (no throw, no retry)
apps/platform/analytics/src/services/sqs-consumer.ts exports createSqsConsumer(pool: Pool): { start(): Promise<void>; stop(): Promise<void> }

sqs-consumer wraps createEventBus() from @ortho/event-bus; subscribes all 13 known event types by calling bus.subscribe(eventType, handler) where each handler calls routeEvent wrapped in p-limit(SQS_CONCURRENCY)
SQS_CONCURRENCY defaults to 5 when env var is not set
sqs-consumer calls bus.start() on start() and bus.stop() on stop()
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The 13 event types to subscribe: lead.created, lead.stage_changed, lead.archived, lead.converted, message.delivered, message.failed, opt_out.received, campaign.sent, campaign.delivered, email.opened, email.clicked, referral.converted, ad_spend.synced. Unknown types are handled in the default case of the switch in event-router.ts.

userStories.title: API key auth plugin (src/plugins/api-key-auth.ts)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pre-middleware Fastify plugin that handles ak_-prefixed API keys so that the Reporting Service can authenticate to Analytics Service endpoints without a JWT.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/plugins/api-key-auth.ts exports a Fastify plugin registered with fastify-plugin
Plugin adds an onRequest hook that fires before @ortho/auth-middleware
If Authorization header starts with 'ak_': extracts the key, computes SHA256(key), checks an in-memory LRU/Map cache keyed by the hash

On cache miss: calls POST IDENTITY_SERVICE_URL/identity/api-keys/validate with the key in the body; caches the valid result for API_KEY_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS (default 60); on 401/403 response rejects the request with 401
On cache hit: skips Identity Service call, uses cached result
Injects X-User-Role and X-Api-Key-Permissions headers from the validation response so downstream auth middleware sees a consistent context
If Authorization header does NOT start with 'ak_': plugin does nothing and passes to the next hook
API_KEY_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS env var controls cache TTL
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do NOT modify @ortho/auth-middleware — this plugin is Analytics Service-specific. Use Node's built-in crypto.createHash('sha256') for the cache key. A simple Map with TTL (or lru-cache) is sufficient — no Redis needed for the auth cache.

userStories.title: Fastify server setup — env, app, health/ready routes, index.ts, worker.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the complete Fastify server wiring with environment validation, health endpoints, and dual-mode entry points so that the service can be started as an embedded HTTP+consumer process or as a standalone SQS worker.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/env.ts validates and exports all env vars from spec Section 7: DATABASE_URL (required), SQS_QUEUE_URL (required), IDENTITY_SERVICE_URL (required), REDIS_URL (required), ADMIN_RECOMPUTE_KEY (required), PORT (optional, default 3000), LOG_LEVEL (optional, default info), API_KEY_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS (optional, default 60), SQS_POLLING_INTERVAL_MS (optional), SQS_BATCH_SIZE (optional, default 10), SQS_CONCURRENCY (optional, default 5)

apps/platform/analytics/src/routes/health.ts: GET /health always returns 200 { status: 'ok' }; GET /ready checks DB connectivity with a lightweight query (SELECT 1) and returns 200 { status: 'ready' } or 503 { status: 'unavailable', reason: string }

apps/platform/analytics/src/app.ts exports buildApp(pool: Pool): Promise<FastifyInstance> that registers @fastify/sensible, api-key-auth plugin, @fastify/rate-limit, and health routes; other route registrations will be added in subsequent stories

apps/platform/analytics/src/index.ts starts the HTTP server and the SQS consumer concurrently (not via Fastify hooks); handles SIGTERM gracefully by stopping the consumer and closing the server and pool
apps/platform/analytics/src/worker.ts starts only the SQS consumer (no HTTP server); handles SIGTERM by stopping the consumer and ending the pool
GET /health returns 200 { status: 'ok' } (verifiable via app.inject in test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: worker.ts is the standalone ECS task entry point for deployments that separate ingestion from the API. index.ts is the default embedded mode. Both share the same sqs-consumer module from US-011.

userStories.title: Named metric routes — leads, pipeline, conversions, messages
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the first four named metric endpoints so that the Reporting Service can query lead counts, pipeline funnel data, conversion rates, and messaging delivery stats.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/routes/metrics/leads.ts: GET /analytics/metrics/leads accepts period, granularity (daily|monthly|total, default daily), location_id[], channel[], page (default 1), page_size (default 1000, max 1000); queries metrics_leads_daily; returns { period, granularity, data: [...], meta: { total, page, page_size, total_pages } }
apps/platform/analytics/src/routes/metrics/pipeline.ts: GET /analytics/metrics/pipeline, same shared params plus pipeline and stage filters; queries metrics_pipeline_daily
apps/platform/analytics/src/routes/metrics/conversions.ts: GET /analytics/metrics/conversions, same shared params plus channel filter; queries metrics_conversions_daily
apps/platform/analytics/src/routes/metrics/messages.ts: GET /analytics/metrics/messages, same shared params; queries metrics_messages_daily; response rows include delivered, failed, opt_outs
All four routes validate input with @sinclair/typebox schemas and return 400 on validation errors
period parameter accepts YYYY-MM (calendar month) or YYYY-MM-DD/YYYY-MM-DD (custom range); returns 400 for invalid formats
Routes registered in app.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: granularity=daily returns one row per date per dimension group; granularity=monthly aggregates to YYYY-MM; granularity=total returns a single aggregate row. Use SQL GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC for monthly and no date grouping for total. All routes are in the platform_analytics schema.

userStories.title: Named metric routes — ad-spend (+ /campaigns sub-route), campaigns, referrals, coordinators
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the remaining four named metric endpoints so that ad performance, email campaign stats, referral counts, and coordinator activity data are all queryable.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/routes/metrics/ad-spend.ts: GET /analytics/metrics/ad-spend with platform and campaign_id filters; GET /analytics/metrics/ad-spend/campaigns always groups by campaign_id (prevents misuse of campaign_name display hint); both accept shared period/granularity/location_id/page/page_size params
apps/platform/analytics/src/routes/metrics/campaigns.ts: GET /analytics/metrics/campaigns with campaign_id filter; returns sent, delivered, opened, clicked per row
apps/platform/analytics/src/routes/metrics/referrals.ts: GET /analytics/metrics/referrals; queries metrics_referrals_daily

apps/platform/analytics/src/routes/metrics/coordinators.ts: GET /analytics/metrics/coordinators with coordinator_id filter; response rows include coordinator_id, response_time_sum, response_time_count, time_in_stage_sum, time_in_stage_count (raw sums — Reporting Service computes means)
All four routes use shared query param validation; return { period, granularity, data, meta } envelope
All four routes registered in app.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The /ad-spend/campaigns sub-route must always GROUP BY campaign_id regardless of other params — this enforces the constraint documented in the migration and handler.

userStories.title: Generic query DSL — query-builder.ts + POST /analytics/query route
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure-function query builder and the POST /analytics/query endpoint so that the Reporting Service and other callers can run ad-hoc queries against the raw analytics_events table.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/services/query-builder.ts exports buildQuery(params: QueryParams): { sql: string; bindings: unknown[] } as a pure function — no DB calls, no side effects

buildQuery supports: aggregate (count | sum | avg), aggregate_field (dot-notation into dimensions/properties jsonb), filters (equality or IN matching with dot-notation keys, all ANDed), group_by (array of dot-notation fields), granularity (daily | monthly | total), period ({ from: string, to: string })

apps/platform/analytics/src/routes/query.ts: POST /analytics/query validates body with typebox, calls buildQuery, executes against analytics_events, caps result at 10000 rows, returns { rows, total, truncated: boolean }
Route registered in app.ts
Rate limit: JWT callers 10 req/min (keyed by JWT sub); API key callers 100 req/min (keyed by SHA256(key)); applied via @fastify/rate-limit per-route config
Returns 400 for invalid body (missing event_type, invalid aggregate, etc.)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: query-builder.ts is a pure function so it can be unit-tested exhaustively without a DB. Dot-notation mapping: 'dimensions.channel' → jsonb operator dimensions->>'channel'. group_by fields map to the same JSONB extraction syntax in the SQL GROUP BY clause.

userStories.title: BullMQ jobs — partition maintenance and recompute rollups processor
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the two BullMQ job processors registered so that monthly partition creation and admin-triggered rollup recomputes execute as background jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/jobs/partition-maintenance.ts exports registerPartitionMaintenanceJob(queue: Queue, pool: Pool): void that schedules a BullMQ repeatable job (cron '1 0 1 * *') under queue prefix analytics:; job logic: creates next month partition (e.g. analytics_events_2026_05), moves rows from analytics_events_default into it if they fall in range, drops the partition 25 months ago

apps/platform/analytics/src/jobs/recompute-rollups.ts exports a BullMQ Worker processor that reads table and date_range from job.data; deletes rows in that table for the date range; re-inserts by aggregating from analytics_events raw log; updates job progress
Recompute processor returns { rows_written: number } on completion
Both jobs use REDIS_URL env var for the BullMQ connection; queue name prefixed with analytics:
Jobs registered in index.ts (not in worker.ts — job scheduling is tied to the HTTP server process)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The recompute job must support all 8 rollup table names as valid values for job.data.table. For partition-maintenance: the partition names follow the pattern analytics_events_YYYY_MM. Use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS analytics_events_YYYY_MM PARTITION OF analytics_events FOR VALUES FROM ('YYYY-MM-01') TO ('YYYY-MM+1-01').

userStories.title: Admin recompute REST endpoints
userStories.description: As an operator, I need POST /analytics/admin/recompute and GET /analytics/admin/recompute/:job_id so that I can trigger and monitor rollup recomputation without direct DB access.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/src/routes/admin.ts: POST /analytics/admin/recompute validates X-Admin-Key header against ADMIN_RECOMPUTE_KEY env var; returns 401 if missing or wrong
POST body validated: { table: one of 8 rollup table names, date_range: { from: string (YYYY-MM-DD), to: string (YYYY-MM-DD) } }; returns 400 on invalid body
POST enqueues a BullMQ job to the analytics: queue and returns 202 { job_id: string }
GET /analytics/admin/recompute/:job_id validates X-Admin-Key; reads job from BullMQ; returns { job_id, status: pending|active|completed|failed, rows_written: number|null, error: string|null }
Both routes registered in app.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The admin endpoints are protected by X-Admin-Key header only (shared secret, no JWT required). ADMIN_RECOMPUTE_KEY is a required env var validated in env.ts.

userStories.title: Unit tests — all 13 event handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for each of the 13 event handlers so that rollup logic, idempotency gates, and edge cases are verified without a real database.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/test/unit/handlers/ contains one test file per handler (13 files)
Each test mocks the pg Pool/PoolClient, mocking pool.connect(), client.query(), client.release()
For each handler: assert the correct analytics_events insert SQL is called with expected params; assert the correct rollup upsert SQL is called with expected dimension values
Idempotency: when insertEvent returns inserted=false (simulate ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING returning rowCount=0), assert the rollup upsert is NOT called

stage-changed handler: assert coordinator rollup is skipped when triggered_by is absent; assert response_time increment is skipped when response_time_seconds is absent; assert both pipeline AND coordinator rollups are skipped on duplicate event_id
ad-spend-synced handler: assert upsertAdSpendDaily IS called even when insertEvent returns inserted=false (relaxed idempotency)
campaign-delivered handler: assert delivered is incremented by recipient_count (e.g. 842), not by 1
referral-converted handler: assert both metrics_referrals_daily and metrics_conversions_daily upserts are skipped on duplicate
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Mock the repositories module (events.ts and rollups.ts) using vi.mock or pass mock implementations. The handlers accept a Pool parameter, so inject a mock Pool in tests.

userStories.title: Unit tests — query-builder.ts (SQL generation) and api-key-auth.ts (cache behaviour)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for the query builder pure function and the API key auth plugin so that SQL generation correctness and auth caching are verified without network calls.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/test/unit/query-builder.test.ts: tests for aggregate=count (no aggregate_field needed), aggregate=sum with aggregate_field, aggregate=avg with aggregate_field

query-builder tests: filters with single value, filters with multiple values (IN), group_by with one and two fields, granularity=daily/monthly/total, period date range applied correctly as WHERE occurred_at BETWEEN clauses
query-builder tests: assert generated SQL strings contain expected fragments and bindings array contains expected values
apps/platform/analytics/test/unit/api-key-auth.test.ts: mock fetch/http call to Identity Service; assert first call with ak_-prefixed key hits Identity Service and caches result

api-key-auth tests: second call with same key uses cache (Identity Service called only once); assert TTL expiry causes re-validation (simulate by manipulating cache); assert non-ak_ Authorization header bypasses the plugin
Tests pass
userStories.notes: query-builder.ts is a pure function — no mocking needed for its tests, just call buildQuery() with params and assert the returned { sql, bindings }. For api-key-auth, mock the HTTP call to IDENTITY_SERVICE_URL using vi.mock or vi.spyOn on the underlying fetch/http module.

userStories.title: Integration tests — event routing → handler → DB and HTTP endpoint suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests that run against a real Postgres database to verify that the full event ingestion path correctly writes to analytics_events and rollup tables, and that the HTTP API returns correct data.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/platform/analytics/test/integration/event-ingestion.test.ts: uses describe.skipIf(!process.env['DATABASE_URL']); creates schema and all tables inline (no migration runner); routes events through routeEvent() with a real pool; asserts rows in analytics_events and relevant rollup tables
Duplicate event_id test: calling routeEvent twice with the same event_id results in exactly one row in analytics_events and no double-counting in the rollup table
stage-changed integration: assert both metrics_pipeline_daily and metrics_coordinators_daily rows exist after routing a lead.stage_changed event with triggered_by set
ad-spend-synced integration: route same event twice (same event_id), assert metrics_ad_spend_daily was overwritten (not double-counted), assert analytics_events has only one row for that event_id

apps/platform/analytics/test/integration/http.test.ts: uses describe.skipIf(!process.env['DATABASE_URL']); builds the Fastify app with a real pool; seeds analytics_events rows directly; calls POST /analytics/query via app.inject and asserts response shape { rows, total, truncated }
GET /health → 200 { status: 'ok' } and GET /ready → 200 { status: 'ready' } tested via app.inject
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow the AI service integration test pattern: create tables with raw SQL in beforeAll, delete rows in beforeEach, end pool in afterAll. Use MockDriver from @ortho/event-bus for the SQS consumer in integration tests (no real SQS needed). The HTTP tests use app.inject — no real HTTP server needed.
